zh.lifeSummary: 當Mikkel Jensen ( Rud)於大約1354年出生在丹麥口斯奧桑可特 包福爾斯時，他的父親Jens Rud23歲，母親Ailed Grubendal21歲。他於大約1371年與Else結婚。他去世於1402年6月17日在丹麥維斯亞藍德索洛享年49歲，埋葬在丹麥維斯亞藍德瓦爾奧利勒 吉可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Mikkel Jensen ( Rud)于大约1354年出生在丹麦口斯奧桑可特 包福爾斯时，他的父亲Jens Rud23岁，母亲Ailed Grubendal21岁。他于大约1371年与Else结婚。他去世于1402年6月17日在丹麦維斯亞藍德索洛享年49岁，埋葬在丹麦維斯亞藍德瓦爾奧利勒 吉可。

nameMeanings.name: Jensen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Nels, Niels, Lars, Holger, Einer, Jorgen, Bent, Knud, Per, Ove, Morten.

Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Jens, a shortened form of Johannes (see John ). This is the most frequent surname in Denmark. In North America, this surname is also an altered form of the variant Jenssen . Compare Jenson 2 and Johnson .

variants.name: Jenssen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Carsten, Bjorn, Lars, Nels.  German Hans.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: variant of   Jensen  .

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

nameMeanings.name: Rud)
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Hungarian (Rúd): from rúd ‘stick’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a soldier or a nickname for a fighter.
English: variant of   Rudd  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Ruud
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Knut, Nels, Nils, Alf, Egil, Lars, Ludvig, Ottar.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads called Ru(u)d, in eastern Norway particularly, from Old Norse ruth ‘clearing’. Compare Rud , Rood , and Roode .

nameMeanings.name: Mikkel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Michael
meanings: form of a common biblical name (meaning ‘who is like God?’ in Hebrew) borne by one of the archangels, the protector of the ancient Hebrews, who is also regarded as a saint of the Catholic Church. In the Middle Ages, Michael was regarded as captain of the heavenly host (see Revelation 12:7–9), symbol of the Church Militant, and patron of soldiers. He was often depicted bearing a flaming sword. The name is also borne by a Persian prince and ally of Belshazzar mentioned in the Book of Daniel. Since the early 1900s it has been one of the most enduringly popular boys' names in the English-speaking world.  See also  Michal .

historicEvents.description: The Union of Kalmar unites Denmark, Sweden and Norway.

en.lifeSummary: When Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) was born about 1354, in Sankt Povls, Korsør, Denmark, his father, Jens Rud, was 23 and his mother, Ailed Grubendal, was 21. He married Else about 1371. He died on 17 June 1402, in Sorø, Vestsjælland, Denmark, at the age of 49, and was buried in Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Denmark.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) est né environ 1354, à Sankt Povls, Korsør, Danemark, son père, Jens Rud, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Ailed Grubendal, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Else environ 1371. Il est décédé le 17 juin 1402, à Sorø, Vestsjælland, Danemark, à l’âge de 49 ans, et a été enterré à Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Danemark.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) um 1354 in Sankt Povls, Korsør, Dänemark geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jens Rud 23 und seine Mutter Ailed Grubendal 21. Er hat um 1371 Else geheiratet. Er starb am 17. Juni 1402 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Sorø, Vestsjælland, Dänemark und wurde in Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Dänemark begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) è nato il circa 1354 nel Sankt Povls, Korsør, Danimarca, il suo padre, Jens Rud, aveva 23 anni e il suo madre, Ailed Grubendal, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Else circa 1371. Morì il 17 giugno 1402, nel Sorø, Vestsjælland, Danimarca, all'età di 49 anni, e fu sepolto a Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Danimarca.

ja.lifeSummary: Mikkel Jensen ( Rud)が約1354年にデンマークコルスoルサンクト ポvルズで生まれた時，父Jens Rudは23歳，母Ailed Grubendalは21歳であった。約1371年にElseと結婚した。彼は1402年6月17日にデンマークベストスジaeランドソルoで49歳で死去し，デンマークベストスジaeランドヴァルスoリル キルクにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Mikkel Jensen ( Rud)(이)가 대략 1354년 덴마크 코르스o르 산k트 포블스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jens Rud(은)는 23세였고, 그의 어머니 Ailed Grubendal(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1371년 Else(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1402년 6월 17일 덴마크 베스츠jae랜드 소o에서 49세의 나이로 사망했으며 덴마크 베스츠jae랜드 발스o리 커크에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) nasceu aproximadamente 1354, em Sankt Povls, Korsør, Dinamarca, seu pai, Jens Rud, tinha 23 anos e sua mãe, Ailed Grubendal, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Else aproximadamente 1371. Ele faleceu em 17 de junho de 1402, em Sorø, Vestsjælland, Dinamarca, com 49 anos, e foi sepultado em Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Dinamarca.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) родился около 1354 г. в местности санкт повлс, корсoр, Дания, его отцу по имени Jens Rud было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Ailed Grubendal, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Else около 1371 г. Он умер 17 июня 1402 г. в местности сорo, весцджaeлланд, Дания в возрасте 49 лет и был похоронен в местности валсoлилл кирк, весцджaeлланд, Дания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Mikkel Jensen ( Rud) nació aproximadamente 1354, en Sankt Povls, Korsør, Dinamarca, su papá, Jens Rud, tenía 23 años y su mamá, Ailed Grubendal, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Else aproximadamente 1371. Murió el 17 de junio de 1402, en Sorø, Vestsjælland, Dinamarca, a la edad de 49 años, y fue sepultado en Valsølille Kirke, Vestsjælland, Dinamarca.
